name: noisereduce (Audio Noise Reduction)

performance.notes: Fast numpy-based spectral subtraction; near real-time for short clips

agent_readiness.notes: Simple spectral noise reduction library. nr.reduce_noise(y=audio, sr=sample_rate) — one-liner denoising. Stationary noise profile: provide noise sample or auto-detect. Non-stationary noise mode for time-varying noise. PyTorch GPU acceleration available. Works with numpy arrays or torch tensors. Output is cleaned audio array. Used for voice recording cleanup, podcast production, ASR preprocessing. Simple but effective for common noise types.

gotchas: Spectral subtraction can introduce musical noise artifacts
Best for stationary noise (HVAC, hum) — less effective for intermittent noise
Aggressive settings can degrade speech quality
Not as sophisticated as deep learning denoisers (RNNoise, DeepFilterNet)
best_when: Quick noise reduction for recorded audio
ASR preprocessing to improve recognition accuracy
avoid_when: Highest quality denoising (use deep learning methods)
summary: noisereduce provides one-liner audio noise reduction via spectral subtraction. nr.reduce_noise(y=audio, sr=sr) removes stationary background noise. Non-stationary mode for time-varying noise. PyTorch GPU support. Simple, fast, and effective for common noise types. Best for voice recording cleanup, ASR preprocessing, and podcast production. Not as sophisticated as deep learning denoisers but extremely easy to use.
